scenes.speech: Build objects with classes! Programs become organized!
scenes.title: Classes and Objects

scenes.speech: A way of representing the real world in code!
scenes.title: What is OOP?
scenes.content: Create a Dog class, then make individual dog objects like Spot, Rex. Code becomes organized and reusable!

scenes.speech: The basic way to create a class!
scenes.title: Defining a Class
scenes.content: class Dog: defines a class. __init__ method initializes it. Group attributes and behavior together!

scenes.speech: Define a class's data and functionality!
scenes.title: Attributes and Methods
scenes.content: Attributes (self.name) are data, methods (def bark()) are functions. Use self to refer to the instance!

scenes.title: Instantiation
scenes.content: dog = Dog('Spot') creates a Dog instance. Use dog.name and dog.bark() to access attributes and methods!

scenes.title: Inheritance
scenes.content: class Poodle(Dog): inherits from Dog to make a new class. Reuse existing code!

scenes.title: Encapsulation
scenes.content: By convention, attributes starting with _ shouldn't be changed externally. Protects data integrity!

scenes.title: Special Methods
scenes.content: __str__() for display, __len__() for length, __eq__() for comparison. Define your own behaviors!

scenes.speech: Build a card game with classes!
scenes.title: Practical: Card Game
scenes.content: Create Card, Deck, Player classes and build a card game. Feel the power of object-oriented programming!
scenes.highlight: Card + Deck + Player = Card game complete!
